title: A TRIzol-Beadbeater RNA extraction method for yeast
author: Sangeet Honey

steps.title: Harvest 50 mL yeast culture samples...
steps.instruction: Harvest 50 mL yeast culture samples by centrifugation at 3,000 rpm for 5 minutes at 4 °C using a Sorvall Super T21 centrifuge.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant by vacuum aspiration...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant by vacuum aspiration and resuspend each cell pellet in 1 mL TRIzol reagent. Aliquot each resuspended sample into two tubes (each containing 0.5 mL) and store at −80 °C until further processing.

steps.title: For lysis, thaw one set of...
steps.instruction: For lysis, thaw one set of frozen yeast in TRIzol on ice.

steps.title: Transfer thawed samples into 2 mL...
steps.instruction: Transfer thawed samples into 2 mL screw-cap tubes with an O‑ring.

steps.title: Add zirconium oxide beads (0
steps.instruction: Add zirconium oxide beads (0.5 mm diameter) from a plastic weigh boat until the bead level reaches approximately 1 mm below the liquid meniscus.

steps.title: Pre-chill tubes on ice to ~0...
steps.instruction: Pre-chill tubes on ice to ~0 °C, then load tubes in a balanced configuration into an Omni International Bead Ruptor 12.

steps.title: Homogenize at 3
steps.instruction: Homogenize at 3.1 m/s for 30 seconds using the Omni Bead Ruptor 12.

steps.title: Immediately place tubes on ice after homogenization
steps.instruction: Immediately place tubes on ice after homogenization. To assess lysis, take a 1 µL aliquot (avoiding beads), mix with 6 µL of water on a microscope slide, and inspect under phase-contrast microscopy to assess cell disruption (typically >85% of cells are lysed after a single cycle).

steps.title: If lysis is insufficient, perform a...
steps.instruction: If lysis is insufficient, perform a second 30-second homogenization cycle; avoid prolonged agitation to prevent overheating.

steps.title: Transfer the cell lysate to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the cell lysate to a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube using a pipette tip, leaving the zirconium oxide beads behind.

steps.title: Add chloroform equal to 20% of the TRIzol volume (e
steps.instruction: Add chloroform equal to 20% of the TRIzol volume (e.g., 200 µL chloroform per 1 mL TRIzol). Gently rock the mixture for 3 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples at 14,000 rpm...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples at 14,000 rpm for 15 minutes at room temperature in a tabletop centrifuge to effect phase separation.

steps.title: Identify the three phases after centrifugation
steps.instruction: Identify the three phases after centrifugation: a clear upper aqueous phase (containing RNA), a white interphase (containing DNA), and a red lower organic phase (containing proteins). Carefully transfer the aqueous phase to a 15 mL centrifuge tube, avoiding disruption of the interphase.

steps.title: To the recovered aqueous phase, add...
steps.instruction: To the recovered aqueous phase, add 350 µL of Binding Buffer (Ambion RiboPure™ Yeast RNA Purification Kit) per 100 µL of aqueous solution.

steps.title: Add ethanol (200‑proof) to the sample...
steps.instruction: Add ethanol (200‑proof) to the sample to reach a final ethanol concentration of 70% and mix according to the kit instructions.

steps.title: Process the sample using the Ambion...
steps.instruction: Process the sample using the Ambion RiboPure™ Yeast RNA Purification Kit filter cartridges following the manufacturer's protocol.

steps.title: Elute RNA in 2 × 25...
steps.instruction: Elute RNA in 2 × 25 µL of 10 mM Tris‑HCl (pH 8.0).

steps.title: Determine RNA concentration using a NanoDrop spectrophotometer
steps.instruction: Determine RNA concentration using a NanoDrop spectrophotometer.

steps.title: As a quality-control recommendation, measure and...
steps.instruction: As a quality-control recommendation, measure and record the RNA yield for every extracted sample (e.g., using NanoDrop) and report the RNA yield when publishing results.
